messages.text: That afternoon I finished the repair. The engine answered at last, and its rough noise should have filled me with peace.
Instead I found the little prince sitting on a broken wall, speaking to the snake as if to an old appointment.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: Tonight is the anniversary of my fall to Earth. My star will be above this place.

messages.name: Snake
messages.text: I will be there. Your body is too heavy for the journey.

messages.name: Narrator
messages.text: What does he mean by heavy? Why are you talking to that creature?
The snake slipped away. The prince looked pale, but calm, as though he had spent the day learning how to prepare goodbye.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: You have your machine now. You can go home. I can go home too.

messages.name: Narrator
messages.text: Not like this. I will not leave you with poison and riddles.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: It will look like death, but it will not be true. The physical shell cannot travel so far.
He asked me to understand, yet every word made understanding harder. A friend should remain visible. A friend should not fall silent.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: When you look at the stars, one of them will be where I live. All stars will laugh for you.

messages.name: Narrator
messages.text: I do not want laughing stars. I want you here, alive.
He touched my hand. His courage was not a game. He felt responsible for a rose who waited under a glass shelter.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: My rose is precious. I must return. I made a promise without knowing its full weight.
The word sacrifice came to me, though he never used it. He was giving up the comfort of being seen in order to keep faith with what was unseen.
Night rose around us. He walked toward the appointed place with small steady steps, and I followed though my heart resisted each one.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: You must not watch if it frightens you. It will be like an old bark falling from a tree.

messages.name: Narrator
messages.text: I will not leave you alone.
There was a yellow flash near his ankle. He made no cry. He fell gently, as silent as a tree falls in sand.
At dawn, his body was gone. The desert held only tracks, stars, and a peace too large for me to accept.
I wanted to argue like an adult, with reasons, plans, and physical facts. He answered from a place where responsibility mattered more than proof.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: If I remain here, my rose remains alone. If I go, you will think I have vanished. Both things hurt.

messages.name: Narrator
messages.text: Then choose the hurt that lets you live. A promise should not ask for your body.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: The body is only the heavy part. The promise is the part that knows the way.
Those words brought no peace to me. They made his sacrifice sound gentle, which was almost worse than fear.
When he fell, the desert did not shout. Its silence made the moment larger, as if even the stars had agreed to remain still.
I looked for tracks, for any physical sign that would let me deny what happened. The sand offered nothing certain.

messages.name: Narrator
messages.text: If this is peace, it is a peace that hurts.
Yet the absence of his body also carried wonder. The desert had become a border, not merely a grave.
I could not decide whether he was brave or impossibly young. Perhaps he was both, and perhaps love often looks like both at once.

messages.name: Little Prince
messages.text: Do not come with anger in your heart. The stars will need room to laugh.
That request was almost unbearable, because it asked me to prepare for wonder while standing beside fear.
